name: Command Code

form_factor.comment: Terminal coding agent exposed as `cmd`/`command-code`, with IDE integration and Studio/account surfaces. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://commandcode.ai/docs/quickstart; Extension: https://commandcode.ai/docs/ide-integration; Web: https://commandcode.ai/docs/studio.

released_in.comment: Launch announcement presents Command Code as a newly launched coding agent CLI with taste learning.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest public changelog entry v0.43.1 (July 8, 2026) fixes the auto-updater script after v0.43.0 removed a dead bin/run.mjs fallback; v0.42.0 added memory management and sliding session windowing.

pricing.value: Go $1/mo, Pro $15/mo, Max 10x $100/mo, Max 20x $200/mo; monthly credits and top-ups; BYOK/provider options documented
pricing.comment: Pricing page lists Go, Pro, Max 10x, and Max 20x tiers with monthly credits, model access, 1M context, and top-up behavior.

some_free_usage.comment: Docs advertise temporary free model deals, but ordinary agent use requires signing in and a paid/credit-backed plan.

no_account_required.comment: Quickstart requires `cmd login` before normal coding use.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: CLI exposes `/provider` and pricing/docs describe provider/API-key surfaces, so usage can route through non-default providers where configured.

rules.comment: CLI has `/init` for AGENTS.md and Taste/memory features for persistent project guidance.

skills.comment: Supports user-level `~/.commandcode/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md` and project-level `.commandcode/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md` skills plus `cmd skills` management.

hooks.comment: Docs expose a dedicated Hooks section and changelog includes SessionStart hook support.

mcp_servers.comment: `cmd mcp` manages MCP servers; `/mcp` is available in sessions.

custom_commands.comment: Docs list slash commands and a dedicated custom slash commands section.

goal_command.comment: Built-in `/goal` sets an autonomous objective with clear/status behavior.

subagents.comment: `/agents` manages agent configurations; docs include a Custom Agents section.

monitor.comment: Session memory/windowing, traces, status, and checkpoints are documented, but no separate background monitor/watch primitive was confirmed.

transcripts.comment: `/session-file` reveals the current session id and on-disk session path.

approval_mode.comment: Supports plan, standard, auto-accept, and yolo/dangerous skip permission modes.

sandbox_mode.comment: Permission modes and yolo are documented; no separate filesystem/network sandbox selector was found.

non_interactive.comment: Supports `--print [query]` to run non-interactively and exit.

output_format.comment: `cmd status --json` exists; no full JSON event stream for `--print` was confirmed.

statusline.comment: Changelog documents status-bar indicators and CLI status UI features.

telemetry.comment: Changelog documents OpenTelemetry integration and DO_NOT_TRACK opt-out; later changelog notes CLI telemetry.

custom_model_provider.comment: Provider selection and many model families are documented.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Quickstart points users to `?` for all keyboard shortcuts.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

auto_save.comment: Checkpoints and rewind imply session/file state is saved, but no explicit auto-save setting was confirmed.

debugging_output.comment: Trace id, status, info, and verbose/text info modes are documented for support debugging.

context_window.comment: Pricing page lists context windows up to 1M tokens and changelog includes sliding session windowing.

code_formatting.comment: Taste learning can enforce style preferences, but a built-in formatter/linter integration was not confirmed.

git_integration.comment: Supports PR review and PR-comment workflows, and launch/docs describe commits and PRs as built in.

performance_mode.comment: Supports max-turn limits, model switching, and memory windowing; no named performance mode was confirmed.

ui_customization.comment: Research needed. Public docs did not expose enough detail in this run.

hosted_agent.comment: Primary surface is a local CLI; no managed hosted coding-agent runtime was confirmed.

notes: Command Code is an npm-installed CLI coding agent (`cmd`) centered on continuously learned coding taste, with skills, MCP, hooks, custom agents, goal mode, checkpoints, non-interactive print mode, model/provider selection, and account-backed pricing.
